reforms.description: The Italian government introduced an R&D tax credit for incremental investments in R&D for the period 2015-2019, alongside a patent box regime to support firms' investment in R&D, aimed at alleviating financial constraints and encouraging innovation.
reforms.component_name: R&D Tax Credit and Patent Box
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The tax credit and patent box are designed to stimulate private sector investment in R&D, which is expected to enhance productivity and innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform has been actively applied since its introduction in 2015.
reforms.importance_rationale: The introduction of a comprehensive R&D tax credit and patent box regime represents a significant structural change in the innovation support framework.
reforms.source_quote: The Italian government included in the Legge di Stabilità 2015 two measures for supporting firms' investment in R&D: an R&D tax credit and a 'patent box' regime.

reforms.description: The government has implemented measures to improve access to finance for small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs) and infrastructure projects, including the removal of withholding tax for EU investors on certain securities.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving access to finance is expected to enhance the growth potential of SMEs, thereby contributing to overall economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The measures have been actively applied since their introduction in 2015.
reforms.importance_rationale: The measures represent a significant but narrower change aimed at enhancing financial access for SMEs.
reforms.source_quote: The government has taken a number of measures to make it easier or cheaper for finance to flow to small companies or infrastructure projects.

reforms.description: Italy simplified the criteria for creating start-ups to make science, technology, and innovation policy more business-oriented and responsive to the needs of firms, including SMEs.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Simplifying start-up creation is expected to foster innovation and entrepreneurship, contributing positively to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform has been actively applied since its introduction in 2014.
reforms.importance_rationale: The simplification of start-up criteria represents a significant change in the regulatory environment for new businesses.
reforms.source_quote: Simplification of criteria for creating start-ups.

reforms.description: The Italian government introduced a 50% tax credit on R&D expenditure for businesses over the 2014-2016 period to stimulate private sector investment in research and development.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The tax credit is designed to incentivize private sector investment in R&D, which is expected to enhance innovation and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform has been actively applied since its introduction in 2014.
reforms.importance_rationale: The introduction of a targeted tax credit for R&D expenditure represents a significant but narrower change in the funding landscape for research.
reforms.source_quote: 50% tax credit on the R&D expenditure of businesses over the 2014-16 period.
